title: Preview Creative Response
description: Response containing preview links for one or more creatives. Format matches the request: single preview response for single requests, batch results for batch requests.

oneOf.title: PreviewCreativeSingleResponse
oneOf.description: Single preview response - each preview URL returns an HTML page that can be embedded in an iframe

response_type.description: Discriminator indicating this is a single preview response

previews.description: Array of preview variants. Each preview corresponds to an input set from the request. If no inputs were provided, returns a single default preview.

preview_id.description: Unique identifier for this preview variant

renders.description: Array of rendered pieces for this preview variant. Most formats render as a single piece. Companion ad formats (video + banner), multi-placement formats, and adaptive formats render as multiple pieces.

items.title: Preview Render
items.description: A single rendered piece of a creative preview with discriminated output format

render_id.description: Unique identifier for this rendered piece within the variant

preview_url.description: URL to an HTML page that renders this piece. Can be embedded in an iframe.

role.description: Semantic role of this rendered piece. Use 'primary' for main content, 'companion' for associated banners, descriptive strings for device variants or custom roles.

embedding.description: Optional security and embedding metadata for safe iframe integration

recommended_sandbox.description: Recommended iframe sandbox attribute value (e.g., 'allow-scripts allow-same-origin')

requires_https.description: Whether this output requires HTTPS for secure embedding

supports_fullscreen.description: Whether this output supports fullscreen mode

csp_policy.description: Content Security Policy requirements for embedding

render_id.description: Unique identifier for this rendered piece within the variant

preview_html.description: Raw HTML for this rendered piece. Can be embedded directly in the page without iframe. Security warning: Only use with trusted creative agents as this bypasses iframe sandboxing.

role.description: Semantic role of this rendered piece. Use 'primary' for main content, 'companion' for associated banners, descriptive strings for device variants or custom roles.

recommended_sandbox.description: Recommended iframe sandbox attribute value (e.g., 'allow-scripts allow-same-origin')

requires_https.description: Whether this output requires HTTPS for secure embedding

supports_fullscreen.description: Whether this output supports fullscreen mode

csp_policy.description: Content Security Policy requirements for embedding

oneOf.description: Both URL and HTML preview format

render_id.description: Unique identifier for this rendered piece within the variant

output_format.description: Discriminator indicating both preview_url and preview_html are provided

preview_url.description: URL to an HTML page that renders this piece. Can be embedded in an iframe.

preview_html.description: Raw HTML for this rendered piece. Can be embedded directly in the page without iframe. Security warning: Only use with trusted creative agents as this bypasses iframe sandboxing.

role.description: Semantic role of this rendered piece. Use 'primary' for main content, 'companion' for associated banners, descriptive strings for device variants or custom roles.

embedding.description: Optional security and embedding metadata for safe iframe integration

recommended_sandbox.description: Recommended iframe sandbox attribute value (e.g., 'allow-scripts allow-same-origin')

requires_https.description: Whether this output requires HTTPS for secure embedding

supports_fullscreen.description: Whether this output supports fullscreen mode

csp_policy.description: Content Security Policy requirements for embedding

input.description: The input parameters that generated this preview variant. Echoes back the request input or shows defaults used.

macros.description: Macro values applied to this variant

context_description.description: Context description applied to this variant

interactive_url.description: Optional URL to an interactive testing page that shows all preview variants with controls to switch between them, modify macro values, and test different scenarios.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when preview links expire

context.title: Context Object
context.description: Opaque correlation data that is echoed unchanged in responses. Used for internal tracking, UI session IDs, trace IDs, and other caller-specific identifiers that don't affect protocol behavior. Context data is never parsed by AdCP agents - it's simply preserved and returned.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

oneOf.title: PreviewCreativeBatchResponse
oneOf.description: Batch preview response - contains results for multiple creative requests

response_type.description: Discriminator indicating this is a batch preview response

results.description: Array of preview results corresponding to each request in the same order. results[0] is the result for requests[0], results[1] for requests[1], etc. Order is guaranteed even when some requests fail. Each result contains either a successful preview response or an error.

previews.description: Array of preview variants for this creative

role.description: Semantic role of this rendered piece. Use 'primary' for main content, 'companion' for associated banners, descriptive strings for device variants or custom roles.

supports_fullscreen.description: Whether this output supports fullscreen mode

oneOf.title: PreviewCreativeVariantResponse
oneOf.description: Variant preview response - shows what a specific creative variant looked like when served during delivery

response_type.description: Discriminator indicating this is a variant preview response

creative_id.description: Creative identifier this variant belongs to

previews.description: Array of rendered pieces for this variant. Most formats render as a single piece.

manifest.title: Creative Manifest
manifest.description: The rendered creative manifest for this variant — the actual output that was served, not the input assets

format_id.title: Format ID
format_id.description: Format identifier this manifest is for. Can be a template format (id only) or a deterministic format (id + dimensions/duration). For dimension-specific creatives, include width/height/unit in the format_id to create a unique identifier (e.g., {id: 'display_static', width: 300, height: 250, unit: 'px'}).

agent_url.description: URL of the agent that defines this format (e.g., 'https://creatives.adcontextprotocol.org' for standard formats, or 'https://publisher.com/.well-known/adcp/sales' for custom formats)

id.description: Format identifier within the agent's namespace (e.g., 'display_static', 'video_hosted', 'audio_standard'). When used alone, references a template format. When combined with dimension/duration fields, creates a parameterized format ID for a specific variant.

width.description: Width in pixels for visual formats. When specified, height must also be specified. Both fields together create a parameterized format ID for dimension-specific variants.

height.description: Height in pixels for visual formats. When specified, width must also be specified. Both fields together create a parameterized format ID for dimension-specific variants.

duration_ms.description: Duration in milliseconds for time-based formats (video, audio). When specified, creates a parameterized format ID. Omit to reference a template format without parameters.

promoted_offering.description: Product name or offering being advertised. Maps to promoted_offerings in create_media_buy request to associate creative with the product being promoted.

assets.description:
Map of asset IDs to actual asset content. Each key MUST match an asset_id from the format's assets array (e.g., 'banner_image', 'clickthrough_url', 'video_file', 'vast_tag'). The asset_id is the technical identifier used to match assets to format requirements.

IMPORTANT: Full validation requires format context. The format defines what type each asset_id should be. Standalone schema validation only checks structural conformance — each asset must match at least one valid asset type schema.

anyOf.title: Image Asset
anyOf.description: Image asset with URL and dimensions

format.description: Image file format (jpg, png, gif, webp, etc.)

anyOf.title: Video Asset
anyOf.description: Video asset with URL and technical specifications including audio track properties

container_format.description: Video container format (mp4, webm, mov, etc.)

video_codec.description: Video codec used (h264, h265, vp9, av1, prores, etc.)

video_bitrate_kbps.description: Video stream bitrate in kilobits per second

frame_rate.description: Frame rate as string to preserve precision (e.g., '23.976', '29.97', '30')

frame_rate_type.description: Whether the video uses constant (CFR) or variable (VFR) frame rate

hdr_format.description: HDR format if applicable, or 'sdr' for standard dynamic range

moov_atom_position.description: Position of moov atom in MP4 container

has_audio.description: Whether the video contains an audio track

audio_codec.description: Audio codec used (aac, aac_lc, he_aac, pcm, mp3, ac3, eac3, etc.)

audio_bitrate_kbps.description: Audio bitrate in kilobits per second

captions_url.description: URL to captions file (WebVTT, SRT, etc.)

transcript_url.description: URL to text transcript of the video content

audio_description_url.description: URL to audio description track for visually impaired users

anyOf.title: Audio Asset
anyOf.description: Audio asset with URL and technical specifications

container_format.description: Audio container/file format (mp3, m4a, aac, wav, ogg, flac, etc.)

codec.description: Audio codec used (aac, aac_lc, he_aac, pcm, mp3, vorbis, opus, flac, ac3, eac3, etc.)

transcript_url.description: URL to text transcript of the audio content

anyOf.title: VAST Asset
anyOf.description: VAST (Video Ad Serving Template) tag for third-party video ad serving

delivery_type.description: Discriminator indicating VAST is delivered via URL endpoint

url.description: URL endpoint that returns VAST XML

vpaid_enabled.description: Whether VPAID (Video Player-Ad Interface Definition) is supported

duration_ms.description: Expected video duration in milliseconds (if known)

items.title: VAST Tracking Event
items.description: Standard VAST tracking events for video ad playback and interaction

tracking_events.description: Tracking events supported by this VAST tag

captions_url.description: URL to captions file (WebVTT, SRT, etc.)

audio_description_url.description: URL to audio description track for visually impaired users

delivery_type.description: Discriminator indicating VAST is delivered as inline XML content

vpaid_enabled.description: Whether VPAID (Video Player-Ad Interface Definition) is supported

duration_ms.description: Expected video duration in milliseconds (if known)

items.title: VAST Tracking Event
items.description: Standard VAST tracking events for video ad playback and interaction

tracking_events.description: Tracking events supported by this VAST tag

captions_url.description: URL to captions file (WebVTT, SRT, etc.)

audio_description_url.description: URL to audio description track for visually impaired users

url_type.title: URL Asset Type
url_type.description: Type of URL asset: 'clickthrough' for user click destination (landing page), 'tracker_pixel' for impression/event tracking via HTTP request (fires GET, expects pixel/204 response), 'tracker_script' for measurement SDKs that must load as <script> tag (OMID verification, native event trackers using method:2)

description.description: Description of what this URL points to

accessibility.description: Self-declared accessibility properties for this opaque creative

alt_text.description: Text alternative describing the creative content

keyboard_navigable.description: Whether the creative can be fully operated via keyboard

motion_control.description: Whether the creative respects prefers-reduced-motion or provides pause/stop controls

screen_reader_tested.description: Whether the creative has been tested with screen readers

accessibility.description: Self-declared accessibility properties for this opaque creative

alt_text.description: Text alternative describing the creative content

keyboard_navigable.description: Whether the creative can be fully operated via keyboard

motion_control.description: Whether the creative respects prefers-reduced-motion or provides pause/stop controls

screen_reader_tested.description: Whether the creative has been tested with screen readers

anyOf.title: Webhook Asset
anyOf.description: Webhook for server-side dynamic content rendering (DCO)

url.description: Webhook URL to call for dynamic content

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time to wait for response in milliseconds

oneOf.title: Universal Macro
oneOf.description: Standardized macro placeholders for dynamic value substitution in creative tracking URLs. Macros are replaced with actual values at impression time. See docs/creative/universal-macros.mdx for detailed documentation.

enumDescriptions.CLICK_URL: Publisher's click tracking URL (auto-inserted by sales agent)
enumDescriptions.GPP_SID: GPP Section ID(s) indicating applicable privacy framework sections
enumDescriptions.IP_ADDRESS: User IP address (often masked for privacy, may return empty)
enumDescriptions.DEVICE_TYPE: Device category (mobile, tablet, desktop, ctv, dooh)
enumDescriptions.OS: Operating system (iOS, Android, tvOS, Roku, etc.)
enumDescriptions.POD_SIZE: Total ads in the ad break

supported_macros.description: Universal macros that can be passed to webhook (e.g., DEVICE_TYPE, COUNTRY). See docs/creative/universal-macros.mdx for full list.

oneOf.title: Universal Macro
oneOf.description: Standardized macro placeholders for dynamic value substitution in creative tracking URLs. Macros are replaced with actual values at impression time. See docs/creative/universal-macros.mdx for detailed documentation.

enumDescriptions.CLICK_URL: Publisher's click tracking URL (auto-inserted by sales agent)
enumDescriptions.GPP_SID: GPP Section ID(s) indicating applicable privacy framework sections
enumDescriptions.IP_ADDRESS: User IP address (often masked for privacy, may return empty)
enumDescriptions.DEVICE_TYPE: Device category (mobile, tablet, desktop, ctv, dooh)
enumDescriptions.OS: Operating system (iOS, Android, tvOS, Roku, etc.)
enumDescriptions.POD_SIZE: Total ads in the ad break

required_macros.description: Universal macros that must be provided for webhook to function

response_type.title: Webhook Response Type
response_type.description: Expected content type of webhook response

anyOf.title: DAAST Asset
anyOf.description: DAAST (Digital Audio Ad Serving Template) tag for third-party audio ad serving

delivery_type.description: Discriminator indicating DAAST is delivered via URL endpoint

url.description: URL endpoint that returns DAAST XML

duration_ms.description: Expected audio duration in milliseconds (if known)

items.title: DAAST Tracking Event
items.description: Standard DAAST tracking events for audio ad playback and interaction

tracking_events.description: Tracking events supported by this DAAST tag

companion_ads.description: Whether companion display ads are included

transcript_url.description: URL to text transcript of the audio content

delivery_type.description: Discriminator indicating DAAST is delivered as inline XML content

duration_ms.description: Expected audio duration in milliseconds (if known)

items.title: DAAST Tracking Event
items.description: Standard DAAST tracking events for audio ad playback and interaction

tracking_events.description: Tracking events supported by this DAAST tag

companion_ads.description: Whether companion display ads are included

transcript_url.description: URL to text transcript of the audio content

anyOf.title: Promoted Offerings
anyOf.description: Complete offering specification combining brand reference, product selectors, and optional SI agent endpoint. Provides all context needed for creative generation and/or conversational experiences about what is being promoted. When si_agent_url is present, hosts can connect users to conversational experiences about any of the offerings.

brand.title: Brand Reference
brand.description: Brand reference. Resolved to full brand identity (logos, colors, tone, assets) at execution time for creative generation.

domain.description: Domain where /.well-known/brand.json is hosted, or the brand's operating domain

brand_id.title: Brand ID
brand_id.description: Brand identifier within the house portfolio. Optional for single-brand domains.

si_agent_url.description: MCP endpoint URL for the brand's SI agent. When present, hosts can connect users to conversational experiences about any of the offerings. The agent handles si_get_offering lookups and full conversations.

product_selectors.title: Promoted Products
product_selectors.description: Selectors to choose which products/offerings from the brand's product catalog to promote

manifest_gtins.description: GTIN product identifiers for cross-retailer catalog matching. Accepts standard GTIN formats (GTIN-8, UPC-A/GTIN-12, EAN-13/GTIN-13, GTIN-14).

manifest_skus.description: Direct product SKU references from the brand's product catalog

manifest_tags.description: Select products by tags from the brand's product catalog (e.g., 'organic', 'sauces', 'holiday')

manifest_category.description: Select products from a specific category in the brand's product catalog (e.g., 'beverages/soft-drinks', 'food/sauces')

manifest_query.description: Natural language query to select products from the brand's catalog (e.g., 'all pasta sauces', 'organic products under $20')

examples.description: Cross-retailer GTIN matching for commerce media product discovery
Direct SKU selection for specific products from brand catalog
UNION selection: products tagged 'organic' OR 'sauces' OR in 'food/condiments' category from brand catalog
Natural language product selection from brand catalog

data.manifest_query: all Kraft Heinz pasta sauces under $5

offerings.description: Offerings available for promotion. Each offering can include creative assets (via portfolio_ref or inline assets) for traditional ads. When si_agent_url is set at the parent level, hosts can offer conversational experiences about any of these offerings.

items.title: Offering
items.description: A promotable offering from a brand. Can represent a campaign, product promotion, service, or any other thing the brand wants to make available. Offerings can be promoted via traditional creatives (using portfolio_ref or assets) or conversational SI experiences (via si_agent_url at the promoted-offerings level).

offering_id.description: Unique identifier for this offering. Used by hosts to reference specific offerings in si_get_offering calls.

name.description: Human-readable offering name (e.g., 'Winter Sale', 'Free Trial', 'Enterprise Platform')

tagline.description: Short promotional tagline for the offering

valid_from.description: When the offering becomes available. If not specified, offering is immediately available.

valid_to.description: When the offering expires. If not specified, offering has no expiration.

checkout_url.description: URL for checkout/purchase flow when the brand doesn't support agentic checkout.

landing_url.description: Landing page URL for this offering.

assets.description: Assets specific to this offering (images, videos, copy)

items.description: Asset definition using standard asset structure

portfolio_ref.description: Reference to a creative portfolio for this offering. Portfolios contain organized creative assets across formats, enabling consistent ad delivery for this specific offering.

keywords.description: Keywords for matching this offering to user intent. Hosts use these for retrieval/relevance scoring.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

data.name: Free 30-day trial
data.description: Try our carbon measurement platform free for 30 days
data.tagline: Start measuring your digital carbon footprint today

data.name: Enterprise DSP Platform
data.description: Full-featured demand-side platform for enterprise advertisers

asset_selectors.description: Selectors to choose specific assets from the brand's asset library

examples.description: Offerings with SI agent for conversational experiences

offerings.name: Free 30-day trial
offerings.description: Try our platform free for 30 days

examples.description: Offerings with both creatives and SI

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when preview links expire

context.title: Context Object
context.description: Opaque correlation data that is echoed unchanged in responses. Used for internal tracking, UI session IDs, trace IDs, and other caller-specific identifiers that don't affect protocol behavior. Context data is never parsed by AdCP agents - it's simply preserved and returned.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

_bundled.note: This is a bundled schema with all $ref resolved inline. For the modular version with references, use the parent directory.
